concept: Wayfind / Establish and Maintain Direction Through a Place
description: To establish and maintain one's location and direction while moving through a physical or informational place. Wayfinding may draw on landmarks and markers, memory or touch, sound or assistance. 'kulo' (guide) names leading by an agent; 'runaki' keeps the traveler as the one finding the way.
sound_symbolism: Moving 'ru' enters the route, while grounded 'na' holds the traveler's present position. Final 'ki' is the small clear point of a marker found in time.
grammatical_notes: An intransitive verb that may take an instrumental phrase: 'mia roe lo siru runaki.' (I find my way using the markers.) A route or destination can appear in the usual spatial phrases. The event-noun rule supplies wayfinding as an activity.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A public path works differently when its turns can be found without requiring one familiar guide beside every traveler.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The verb preserves the traveler's direction over the act even when another person or device supplies information.

tags.cognition: using cues to establish location and direction
tags.spatial: finding a route through a physical or informational place
